direction_board_metadata.paper: Bag of Dims: Training-Free Mechanistic Interpretability via Dimension-Level Sign Patterns
direction_board_metadata.ranking_scheme: Each seed ranked 1-100 on Expected Impact (EI) and Feasibility (F), product = Priority Score. Seeds are projects (30-200 GPU-hr) stemming from this paper.
ranking_caveats: Language-model seeds assume access to a single GPU and the type cache construction (20 min per model).
Vision/audio seeds assume access to base-size models (86M params).
No seed requires writing a new training loop, but some require human annotation effort.

seeds.seed_name: Full dimension catalog for one LM
seeds.description: Catalogue all 2560 dims at the optimal layer of a 4B model. For each dimension, report: (a) the tokens it most strongly activates for (positive and negative polarity), (b) the categories for which it achieves per-dim AUC > 0.75, (c) the category where it is the single best dim, (d) its monotonicity with token frequency/familiarity. Distribute as an interactive table.
seeds.why_important: The paper's central open problem is 'cataloging what each dimension encodes.' This seed directly addresses that. A complete catalog would transform how the community evaluates feature quality.

seeds.description: Reproduce the main experiments (sign-only prediction, type cache, feature discovery, dimension independence, causal flip) on a 70B+ parameter model (e.g., Llama 3 70B, Qwen 2.5 72B). The type cache costs ~1400 token forward passes × number of tokens per model.
seeds.why_important: The paper's main limitation is scale. Confirming at 70B+ would substantially increase confidence. If the structure degrades at scale, that's equally important to know.
seeds.effort: 2-3 weeks (GPU time ~30-50 hrs). Cache construction is the bottleneck.

seeds.seed_name: SAE overlay: where do SAE features and sign patterns align?
seeds.description: For a model with existing SAEs (e.g., Gemma Scope 2), compare the SAE feature basis against sign-prototype space. Measure: (a) how many SAE features are axis-aligned (sign of SAE encoder weight matches per-dim sign polarity), (b) what fraction of SAE 'dead features' correspond to sign patterns, (c) whether SAE reconstruction error concentrates in sign agreement or magnitude reconstruction.
seeds.why_important: Would bridge the gap between this paper and the active SAE literature. If many SAE features are axis-aligned, training cost could be dramatically reduced.
seeds.effort: 4-6 weeks. Requires running SAE forward passes on the same cache tokens.

seeds.description: Characterize the ~42% of dimensions whose signs flip between type-level and contextual settings. For a set of polysemy cases (e.g., 'bat' as animal vs sports), track: (a) which specific dims flip between the two contexts, (b) whether flipped dims encode global context vs local context, (c) whether flipping a subset of context-sensitive dims shifts the readout between senses.
seeds.why_important: Would directly address how context modulates the sign-based readout and provide a mechanistic account of contextual meaning in the standard basis.
seeds.effort: 3-4 weeks. Requires curated polysemy dataset and targeted intervention.

seeds.description: Design a magnitude-based induction primitive. Since flipping signs toward expected does not induce a concept (confirmation: Table 8 Toward ≈ 0), but having the right signs is necessary for detection, the missing element is magnitude/confidence. Test: (a) scaling up magnitudes of a feature's registered dims during forward pass, (b) adding activation to dims that currently have the 'wrong' sign for the target concept, (c) combining sign-flip (toward) with magnitude scaling.
seeds.why_important: Would complete the causal toolkit (suppress + induce). Enables fine-grained concept control for safety applications.

seeds.seed_name: Time-varying type cache: token frequency effects
seeds.description: Analyze how token frequency and corpus statistics affect per-dim sign patterns. Test: (a) are rare tokens encoded with lower magnitude (less confidence)? (b) do frequent tokens have more registered dimensions per feature? (c) does the D/V ratio limitation (Limitation 4) manifest primarily as a low-frequency token problem?
seeds.why_important: Would clarify the statistical properties of the type cache and identify whether the framework disproportionately fails on rare tokens (a known limitation of many interpretability methods).
seeds.effort: 2-3 weeks. Analysis on existing cache data.

seeds.description: Test whether the same per-dim sign structure appears in non-transformer architectures (e.g., Mamba SSMs, RWKV). If sign-magnitude encoding is a general property of large models trained with gradient descent, it should appear; if it's specific to the residual-stream + FFN + attention architecture, it won't.
seeds.why_important: Would determine whether Bag of Dims is a 'transformer-specific' discovery or a general property of neural language models. Has implications for which models can leverage training-free interpretability.
seeds.effort: 6-10 weeks. Requires setting up inference for non-transformer models.

seeds.seed_name: Multimodal transfer: do CLIP sign patterns cross domains?
seeds.description: Test whether sign patterns discovered in one modality (e.g., text) transfer to another modality (e.g., images) in a vision-language model like CLIP. If a 'cat' concept is encoded by a specific sign pattern in the text encoder, does the image encoder's CLS token for a cat image activate the same pattern?
seeds.why_important: Would extend the universality claim to cross-modal alignment. Important for multi-modal safety applications.
seeds.effort: 6-8 weeks. Requires CLIP inference pipeline and cross-modal dataset.

seeds.description: Build a real-time monitoring system that detects safety-relevant sign patterns (e.g., 'violence', 'self-harm', 'deception') at inference time. Evaluate: (a) latency (sub-millisecond per token), (b) coverage vs existing classifiers, (c) false positive rate, (d) robustness to adversarial input.
seeds.why_important: Practical safety application of the framework. If sign patterns detect features reliably at inference time with near-zero overhead, this is a deployable safety monitor.
seeds.effort: 8-12 weeks. Requires system engineering + safety category annotation.

seeds.description: Analyze existing activation steering methods (e.g., activation addition, representation engineering) in terms of sign patterns. Test: (a) do steering vectors primarily inflate sign agreement with the target concept on feature-registered dims? (b) do they flip sign on context-sensitive dims? (c) can steering be replicated by targeted sign flips alone?
seeds.why_important: Would connect the sign-pattern framework to the active steering literature. If steering reduces to sign-pattern manipulation, it becomes more tractable and predictable.
seeds.effort: 6-8 weeks. Requires implementing steering baselines + sign-pattern analysis.

seeds.description: Measure per-layer pairwise MI at every layer of multiple models to characterize whether any layer exhibits bottleneck-pressure coupling (MI > 0.05 bits) consistent with Elhage's superposition regime. If MI is uniformly low across all layers, this would strengthen the claim that real transformers do not operate in the superposition regime.
seeds.why_important: The paper's claim that superposition doesn't bind in real transformers is one of its most consequential (and counter-claimed) findings. A comprehensive per-layer MI profile would be the definitive test.
seeds.effort: 4-6 weeks. Requires cache construction at every layer for multiple models.

seeds.description: For a single model layer, map every FFN neuron to the sign-prototype features it contributes to (via down_proj weight analysis). Produce: (a) a sparse matrix mapping neurons ↔ features, (b) neuron polysemanticity distribution, (c) an analysis of whether neurons are more or less polysemantic than dimensions. Test the 'sparse activation → non-overlapping writes' hypothesis.
seeds.why_important: Would give a complete mechanistic picture of how features are written at one layer. Testing the paper's hypothesis about why dimensions are independent.
seeds.effort: 8-12 weeks. Requires full static weight analysis + clustering.
